tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh hung in an infinite 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.' loop after the heredoc fed answers; falling back to manual project-skeleton stubbing per runbook lines 113-116. Heredoc fed (project_name, desc, platform=4, track=3, deployment=1, y, language=7, dir, y) but the first prompt evidently expected a 1-4 choice (likely Docker install or another pre-collect_project_info prompt).

tests.name: phase_state_json_phase_zero
tests.details: Manually wrote .claude/phase-state.json with current_phase=0 (then advanced to 2 to satisfy upgrade prerequisite).

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT (no --non-interactive flag, prompt-order undocumented)
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh accepts a flagged non-interactive driver, OR documents the exact prompt sequence so heredocs work reliably across hosts.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh enters an infinite 'Invalid choice. Enter a number between 1 and 4.' loop because the heredoc answers misalign with an early choice prompt (likely Docker-install or platform). Required falling back to manual skeleton creation per runbook lines 113-116.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook acknowledges the init.sh-non-scriptable gap (lines 113-116) but there is still no canonical prompt-sequence reference in init.sh itself nor in docs; agents must reverse-engineer it each run.

tests.name: baseline_phase_set
tests.details: Manually advanced phase-state.json current_phase=2 to satisfy 'Phase 2 minimum' upgrade prerequisite (no script automates this).

tests.name: intake_wizard_upgrade_deployment_works
tests.details: Per agent prompt: bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment organizational. Result: 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found.' even though PWD has PROJECT_INTAKE.md. Root cause: intake-wizard.sh:15 sets PROJECT_ROOT="$SCRIPT_DIR/.." (the framework dir) and never honors PWD.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: Workaround: ran scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational (which DOES use cwd-walking find_project_root). Exit 0, completed cleanly after providing tool-preferences.json + intake-progress.json (otherwise CURRENT_TRACK is empty/unreadable from phase-state alone).

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: .current_phase preserved (2 -> 2) and .project preserved (uat-agent-57). However .deployment in phase-state.json was NOT updated — still says 'personal' after a deployment upgrade. intake-progress.json correctly shows 'organizational'. Documents diverged. See bug below.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Upgrade output lists: fill Pre-Phase 0 organizational pre-conditions, complete PROJECT_INTAKE.md Section 8, assign approval authorities.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema, re-ran upgrade. Script proceeded to validation step ('No changes needed — already organizational') without warning or refusing on the sentinel. Upgrade does not consult the BL-015 sentinel.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh header lines 11-19 reference both BL-006 and BL-015 changelog entries.

framework_bugs.summary: intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment unusable: PROJECT_ROOT hardcoded to framework dir
framework_bugs.expected: Script detects project via cwd (like upgrade-project.sh does via find_project_root walking up for .claude/phase-state.json) and runs the upgrade in the project context.
framework_bugs.actual: Errors with 'PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found. Run this script from a Solo Orchestrator project directory.' even when PWD is the project. intake-wizard.sh:15 sets PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)" — always the framework dir, never PWD. Both the runbook (Section F) and the agent prompt direct UAT agents to use this command for deployment upgrades; it is broken on the documented happy path.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh leaves phase-state.json deployment field stale after --deployment upgrade
framework_bugs.reproduction: Personal project at phase 2; bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational. After exit, jq .deployment .claude/phase-state.json still returns 'personal'; intake-progress.json correctly reports 'organizational'.
framework_bugs.expected: Both phase-state.json and intake-progress.json reflect the new deployment after a --deployment upgrade, since phase-state.json is the canonical phase/deployment record.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/upgrade-project.sh:475-507 (the python3 PYEOF block that updates phase-state.json) sets only data['track'] and data['last_upgrade'] — never data['deployment']. Subsequent reads that prefer phase-state.json will see the stale value.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh refuses to read track from phase-state.json (only tool-preferences/intake-progress)
framework_bugs.reproduction: Project with .claude/phase-state.json containing valid track but no .claude/tool-preferences.json or .claude/intake-progress.json. Run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational. Output: 'Current track:  __TRACK__' (placeholder leakage; the script reads through CLAUDE.md fallback). Validation aborts: 'Invalid target track:  __TRACK__'.
framework_bugs.expected: Script falls back to phase-state.json's .track field when tool-preferences/intake-progress are missing — phase-state.json is described in script comments and runbook as the authoritative state file.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/upgrade-project.sh:182-205 reads track only from tool-preferences.json and intake-progress.json. No phase-state fallback. When neither file exists, CURRENT_TRACK ends up empty or polluted with the unfilled __TRACK__ placeholder from CLAUDE.md.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh does not check .claude/pending-approval.json before proceeding
framework_bugs.reproduction: Create .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema, then bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment organizational.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-015 sentinel should block (or at minimum warn loudly about) framework-mutating operations like upgrades, mirroring the pre-commit-gate behavior. Runbook Section F explicitly tests this assertion (pending_approval_sentinel_respected).
framework_bugs.actual: Script proceeds normally with no mention of the pending-approval sentinel.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section F prescribes 'intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment {TARGET_DEPLOYMENT}' as the upgrade entry point, and the agent prompt repeats this. The script does not work from a project directory (see High bug). The runbook should either fix the script or redirect agents to scripts/upgrade-project.sh --deployment <target>.

Runbook says 'private_poc|sponsored_poc) ... --upgrade-deployment {TARGET_DEPLOYMENT}' but --upgrade-deployment only accepts 'personal|organizational' (per intake-wizard.sh:1533 and upgrade-project.sh:23). Going from personal to private_poc requires a separate flag/action that is not documented in Section F.

phase-state.json is described as canonical in many places (e.g. find_project_root in upgrade-project.sh:35-46) but track and deployment are read from other files. There is no documented rule for which file is authoritative for which field.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Grep matches in git ls-files are all from CI workflow templates (e.g. 'secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN' references) and platform-modules/web.md — no actual credentials.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred during the run, .claude/process-audit.log absent as expected.

summary: Upgrade flow personal->organizational succeeded via scripts/upgrade-project.sh, but the runbook-prescribed entry point intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment is broken (PROJECT_ROOT wired to framework dir), phase-state.json's deployment field is left stale after upgrade, the upgrade script ignores phase-state.json for track lookup, and the BL-015 pending-approval sentinel is not consulted by the upgrade path.
